data.content: I can see the project — it's an MSTest project on .NET 8. I'll start `dotnet watch test` which will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file change.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

data.reasoningText:
The user has a .NET 8 MSTest project. I can see one test will fail: `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m` for "express".

They want hot reload set up with `dotnet watch test` so they can iterate. Let me start `dotnet watch test` in async mode so they can see the failures and edit files while it auto-reruns.

arguments.description: Start dotnet watch test for hot reload

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-aa15bbd4929f4ee5ae13a012e7e89c0a && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload", "description": "Start dotnet watch test for hot reload", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for file changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — **passed**
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — **failed** (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher will **automatically re-run all tests** every time you save a change to any file. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` from `10.00m` to `15.00m` (line 24 of `ShippingCalculatorTests.cs`). The watcher will pick it up instantly.
